mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, increasing corporate earnings prospects and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-GDP periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor markets, prompting wage inflation that raises corporate costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03 in any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicate stagflationary pressures, causing equity market sell-offs as growth and inflation risks materialize, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 5%, raise discount rates and suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS) above 150k per month signals robust labor demand, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in high-PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weak supply chain activity, reducing corporate profits and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 100, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand fundamentals, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: The interaction between HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict SPX returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) during monetary tightening cycles suppresses real interest rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
